translations.descricao: Urinary tract infection (UTI) screening is essential for early identification in at-risk populations, such as pregnant women and patients in intensive care units. The prevalence is high in adult women.
translations.sus.indicacao: Screening recommended for pregnant women and patients with risk factors in hospital units.
translations.sus.populacaoAlvo: Pregnant women; adults in ICU with urinary catheterization; women with recurrence.
translations.sociedadesMedicas.indicacao: The American Diabetes Association (2024) and similar societies recommend screening in vulnerable populations, adapted for UTI in metabolic risk contexts.
translations.epidemiologia.prevalencia: The prevalence of UTI in adult women is approximately 50-60% over a lifetime. In Brazil, it is estimated at 10-15% in hospitalized populations.
translations.epidemiologia.incidencia: The annual incidence in premenopausal women is 10-20%. In ICU patients, it can reach 25%.
translations.epidemiologia.mortalidade: Direct mortality from complicated UTI is low (1-2%), but high in sepsis (20-40%). In Brazil, it contributes to 5% of hospital deaths related to infections.
